pattern: Deciding whether one string is a recursive partition-and-optional-swap of another is a memoized split search over every cut point in both the swapped and unswapped orientation.
hints: The scramble operation recursively splits a string into two pieces and may swap their order, so equality must be checked across every possible split position.
At each split index, the two halves are either matched directly to the corresponding halves of the other string, or matched crossed over after a swap.

Memoize on the pair of substrings (or on start indices and length); prune splits early by requiring both candidate halves to share the same character multiset, then recurse on the matched-and the swapped-halves.

edge_inputs.description: a true scramble produced by swapping subtrees
same letters but not a valid scramble
two chars cannot scramble into a non-swap mismatch

stress.description: build s2 as a genuine random scramble of a short s1 so the answer is true
stress.note: constructed scrambles keep the true-answer path exercised; short length keeps the recursion fast
